author: g5n-dev
title: Unsafe Deserialization via Unvalidated File Writes in safe_write_text
notes: Nearly all technical claims are factually incorrect. (1) Permission claim wrong: os.replace() preserves the 0600 permissions from mkstemp — verified empirically; files do NOT inherit umask. (2) TOCTOU claim wrong: mkstemp generates random filenames, not predictable ones. (3) Symlink claim wrong: os.replace() replaces the symlink entry itself rather than following it — the target file is unaffected. (4) Content validation is not the job of a utility write function. (5) The exception IS re-raised after cleanup, so failure is not silent. The function implements a standard atomic-write pattern correctly.
